ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kimberly Shields for (petitioners) Claire Ota, Georgia Gross & Rochelle Petruccelli. Timothy Chambers for (respondent) Elaine Van Vleck.

The Court asked counsel where things stand. Ms. Shields reported that after the June 6, 2016 hearing they moved into trial mode, that they will be filing a Motion to Compel and that Ms. Van Vleck's deposition is set for August 1, 2016 although they have not been able to confirm that she will appear. Mr. Chambers stated that he needs to confer with his client, that he does not think there will be an issue, that Ms. Van Vleck has been cooperating that that she knows that her deposition will be taken. The Court asked for progress on the outstanding documents and is told that the fiduciary expects another 6-8 weeks. Mr. Chambers stated that they cannot go to trial until the accounting is filed and objections are made, that the allegations are tied to the accounting, that if they go to trial without the accounting there will not be enough for the court, that the fiduciary is working diligently, that she has Power of Attorney for Ms. Van Vleck, that they are dependent on the banks producing the documents and that they think the trial date should be set back. The Court asked about the sanctions. Ms. Shields stated that they have not received a signed order from the last hearing. The Court requested that she re-submit it with the amount contemplated. Ms. Shields stated that the sanctions started accruing on July 11, 2016 and are now up to $100.00 per day. Mr. Chambers stated that his client is being penalized enough, that it is not reasonable to increase the sanctions and that she has agreed to pay for the accounting. Both counsel agreed that there was no reason for a further hearing prior to trial and the status on the accounting was taken off calendar.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Continued To Jul-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kimberly Shields, for and with petitioner Clare Ota; Tim Chambers (by CourtCall) for reapondent Elaine Van Vleck.

Mr. Chambers stated that he had filed a declaration last week with a partial first year (2010) accounting, and was having difficulty getting the necessary bank statements to complete it. He is working on the 2011 accounting now as well. Ms. Shields responded that the accounting was due by May 27, 2016. She stated that the transfers that would be reflected in the bank statements were made by Ms. Van Vleck, so that she should have control of that accounting information. Ms. Shields said that sanctions are accruing at the rate of $50 per day since May 27. She requested that she would like to see the first accounting completeed within two weeks, and have sanctions increased to $100 per day thereafter until the accounting is filed. Mr. Chambers replied that he is working diligently with the banks to obtain the needed iinformation, but that 90% has already been provided. He stated that he thought he would have two years' accounting completed in 2-3 months. Ms. Shields stated that in order to be prepared for trial on the date now set, she would need to have the accoiunting information in hand by July. The Court ordered the matter continued to July 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, with sanctions to increase to $100 per day if the accounting is not on file by then. Ms. Shields will prepare an order for submission to the court.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Mar-28-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kim Shields for and with Clare Ota. Timothy Chambers for and with Elaine Van Vleck.

Ms. Shields stated that they need a show cause hearing today, that they have not received the accounting and that they request sanctions. Mr. Chambers stated that they had subpoenaed bank statements some time ago, that on March 4, 2016 they received statements from Wells Fargo, that they have a CPA retained but due to tax season it is impossible to complete the accounting, that they have no control over Wells Fargo and that his client will be paying for the accounting. The Court stated that there was a court order and that something should have been filed indicating that they were unable to comply with the date. Mr. Chambers stated that they have sent copies of all records to opposing counsel. The court stated that even if it were to grant the request for a show cause hearing they would need compliance and asked if it would be better to have the threat of sanctions to get it completed. Ms. Shields stated that they do not agree regarding Wells Fargo not cooperating, that there has been nothing but delays, that Ms. Van Vleck has been represented since September and that the last hearing was in January. The Court stated that it will recess this matter until the end of the calendar and requested that the parties meet and confer during the break. The matter resumed with all parties present and the Court asked if we have the hearing today, what would be proposed and is there any basis for a preliminary production. Mr. Chambers reported that they had a discussion, that they have been proving copies of documents to counsel as they receive them and that they agreed on a deadline for the end of May. Ms. Shields stated that they propose that they have the accounting no later than May 27, 2016, that they request that the court issue an order of sanctions in the amount of $50.00 per day if not received, that they would like a status hearing set for early June with a trial date set for August 29, 2016. Mr. Chambers stated that they agree to the sanctions if they do not comply with the May 27, 2016 deadline and requested that the trial date be in September. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on September 12, 2016. A status on compliance with the May 27, 2016 deadline for an accounting was set for June 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Continued To Mar-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kimberly Shields for Clare Ota (present in court), Georgia Gross, Rochelle Petrucelli and (successor trustee) Albert DaCunha who is present. Tim Chambers for (respondent) Elaine Van Vleck.

The Court asked counsel to address the venue issue. Ms. Shields stated that the challenges must be raised at the outset or waived and that venue was proper when the proceedings started. Mr. Chambers stated that they have no objections to venue, that the parties have reached an agreement that Ms. Van Vleck will prepare an accounting from 7/09 through present, that they subpoenaed bank records in November and expect to have all of them in by the end of this month, that they have hired a CPA and expect to be able to file the accounting in two months. Ms. Shields stated that they want it filed sooner, that they were promised copies of the statements but have not received them and request that they receive copies within 5 days. Mr. Chambers stated that he thought copies had been provided but will make sure, that there is no issue in sending them copies, that this is 6 years of an accounting with bank statements and two properties that were sold and that it is not fair to the CPA to request a shorter time frame. Ms. Shields requested that the court sign an order stating that documents are to be delivered to them by January 29, 2016 and the court agreed. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Everson for and with (petitioner) Clare Ota.

The Court stated that it is 9:30 a.m., 30 minutes after the 9:00 a.m. start of this proceeding, that there is no appearance by Ms. Van Vleck pursuant to the order filed on May 12, 2015, which was an order to appear that was personally served on Ms. Van Vleck. A $50,000.00 bench warrant will be issued. The court recounted the following history:
On 9/19/14 there was an order issued removing Ms. Van Vleck as trustee. On 10/6/14 and Order to Appear was issued for 10/27/14 which was continued several times with no appearance by Ms. Van Vleck. On 11/18/14 an Order to Show Cause re Contempt was issued for appearance on 1/12/15. On 1/12/15 an Order to Appear or Face Bench Warrant for Arrest was issued with monetary sanctions in the amount of $1250.00 for violations to date plus $50.00 per day to continue, with an appearance required on 2/2/15. Ms. Van Vleck appeared on 2/2/15 and agreed to produce all documents. On 2/6/15 an order was issued to produce all documents by 2/9/15 in which a small production of documents was provided to Ms. Everson. On 4/28/15 a detailed order was issued to Ms. Van Vleck to appear on 5/11/15 and turn over all documents in her custody with a definition given of what documents consist of and that if she failed to appear on this date the court will consider all appropriate sanctions, including contempt. Ms. Van Vleck did not appear on that date, an order setting contempt hearing for 5/22/15 was issued and personally served on Ms. Van Vleck. Ms. Everson is to prepare an order and a $50,000.00 Bench Warrant will be issued with said order.
